title: Stack the sheets
intro: Your cell needs 150 µA of drive current, but the footprint is frozen — you can't make the device wider on the wafer. FinFETs grow drive by adding fins; nanosheets grow it by stacking channels vertically, like floors in a parking garage. Build enough effective width into the same footprint.
explain: This is GAA's killer feature: effective width = number of sheets × sheet perimeter, so drive current scales VERTICALLY. Real 3 nm/2 nm-class nanosheet devices stack 3–4 sheets, and the gate wrapping every sheet keeps leakage in check while you stack — your FinFET at 4 fins delivered less current AND more leakage than the 4-sheet stack. Bonus: sheet width is a design knob fins never had.
